text:
Market today: Mixed signals. AI chip news from Alphabet is a strong positive for your tech/chip heavy book, but rising Treasury yields and Middle East tensions could add pressure.

Your holdings: SOXL (-43.71%) and SPAL (-48.91%) are your biggest concerns. Both are leveraged ETFs, decay over time, and are deep in the red. SPAL is a new, highly volatile leveraged ETF. SMH (-11.1%) also needs attention. Remember, margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on Treasury yields; continued rises could weigh on growth stocks. Also, watch for any further news on AI chip development across the sector.

Bottom line: Manage your deep red leveraged positions aggressively while selectively looking for strength in core tech.
plays: SOXL: Consider a significant trim or exit given the -43.71% loss, leveraged decay, and "SELL" status. The listed stop of 200 is not protective at current prices.
SPAL: Evaluate SPAL's -48.91% loss. As a new, leveraged ETF, its volatility and decay demand immediate attention.
MSFT: If looking to add to tech exposure on AI chip news, MSFT has a scanner long setup: entry 392.07-391.03, stop 389.07.
